items.title: After dropping back-to-back games, Tyler Marsh described the Sky’s last few practices as “direct and honest.”
items.author: Drew
items.body_excerpt:
After dropping back-to-back games, Tyler Marsh described the Sky’s last few practices as “direct and honest.”

“There’s an understanding that we’re better than we’ve played in four of the last six quarters.”

The Sky believe they’ve drifted from their defensive identity.

items.title: A No. 1 pick hasn't won the MVP since LeBron James did it in 2013. That ended a stretch of five straight MVP awards for a top pick. This decade is so far out of whack with NBA history. Just one of the last 8 MVP award winners was even a top-10 pick.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt: A No. 1 pick hasn't won the MVP since LeBron James did it in 2013. That ended a stretch of five straight MVP awards for a top pick. This decade is so far out of whack with NBA history. Just one of the last 8 MVP award winners was even a top-10 pick.

items.author: Lucas Kaplan
items.body_excerpt:
no coach is above criticism or disagreement or whatever but I feel bad for Chris DeMarco rn

he doesn't know who's available until 30 seconds before tip-off, the whole roster is either hurt/playing bad/just arrived in the country

he's essentially been in scramble mode from day 1

items.title: May 27, 2004: This and-1 slam by Shaquille O’Neal during the Lakers’ 92-85 win over the Timberwolves in Game 4 of the WCF.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 27, 2004: This and-1 slam by Shaquille O’Neal during the Lakers’ 92-85 win over the Timberwolves in Game 4 of the WCF.

O’Neal had 19 PTS/19 REB/3 BLK and Kobe Bryant scored 31 PTS as Los Angeles took a 3-1 series lead. Minnesota’s Kevin Garnett had 28 PTS/13 REB/9 AST.

items.title: May 27, 1979: Gus Williams (31 PTS) and Dennis Johnson (17 PTS/9 AST/9 REB) lead Seattle to a 105-95 win over the Washington Bullets in Game 3 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 27, 1979: Gus Williams (31 PTS) and Dennis Johnson (17 PTS/9 AST/9 REB) lead Seattle to a 105-95 win over the Washington Bullets in Game 3 of the NBA Finals.

Williams and Johnson would combine to average 52 of the SuperSonics' 101 PPG over the five games of the 1979 Finals.

items.title: Funny thing (or maybe idiosyncratic) as NBA owners prepare to vote on the new draft lottery proposal tomorrow: The No. 1 pick has become more attenuated from getting MVP-level players recently. Victor Wembanyama was the first No. 1 pick to finish top 3 since 2020.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
Funny thing (or maybe idiosyncratic) as NBA owners prepare to vote on the new draft lottery proposal tomorrow: The No. 1 pick has become more attenuated from getting MVP-level players recently. Victor Wembanyama was the first No. 1 pick to finish top 3 since 2020.
